metadata.title:
DENNIS DEAL


KNOCKED OUT

CITY HAS NO RIGHT TO GIVE


BIG PIECE OF WORK ON
SPRAGUE ST. TRESTLE TO
INTERESTED CORPORATION.

pages.raw_text:
An opinion submitted to the
council last night by its legal ad-
wiser, H. l.. Hamblen, settled the
attempts of George E. Dennis to
secure payment of about $4,000
from the city for the Improvement
of property on E. Sprague av. by
filling in the trestle. The opinion
was to the effect that the city had
no right to give Dennis company
the contract without bids first be-
ing called for.


Still Mr.. Dennis was unsatisied
and harangued the council for
about 20 minutes, repeating over
and over again the financial re-
liability of his corporation, sim
cerity in the welfare of the city,
and finally admitted that his de-
sires were prompted mostly on ac-
count of enhancing of the value OF
his holdings. Despite the opinion
of the corporation counsel, he ask
ed the council again to pay him
$4,000 to put in the fill Someone
told him they were already aware
that this was illegal. He subsidized
then on that subject Dennis was
asked how much of the expense
was to be borne by the w. w. P.
and the Inland Empire system,
and in reply said the former had
agreed to pay $1,000 as its share
and the latter would pay half the
expense of the steel trestle over
its tracks, which would amount to
about $12 $500.


Then arose the question Of how
much the city could force those
companies to advance if the city
does the work. The corporation
counsel's opinion held that, accord
ing to the franchise of the w. w.
P. it was compelled to pave, ma
cadamize or otherwise improve"
the bridge. From this the coun-
sci took it that the company could
be forced TO pay the actual cost of
the work between its rails and for
